name: Republic of Biafra (1967–1970)

colors.symbolism: the blood of those killed in the pogroms preceding independence
mourning the dead and the African race
the prosperity and abundance of the land
the rising sun and a glorious future

description: Three horizontal stripes — red, black, green — charged centrally with a rising yellow half-sun bearing eleven rays above a black horizon line.
history: Flag of the Republic of Biafra, the secessionist state in southeastern Nigeria (declared 30 May 1967 by Igbo leader C. Odumegwu Ojukwu). The Biafran War (1967–1970) ended with Biafra's defeat and reincorporation into Nigeria. The eleven rays of the sun represented the eleven provinces of Biafra.

features.symbolism: the eleven provinces of Biafra and the dawn of a new nation
